note: worktree was dirty at task creation

Decision: changes_requested (revision 1). Independent validation: typecheck passed; HTTP contract test 25/25 passed after rerun with elevated local IPC/server permission; application 113/113, source 70/70, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30 passed; guard:worktree and git diff --check passed. Passing tests do not satisfy the T0041 brief. Blockers: 1) HTTP route hardcodes DEFAULT_ALGORITHM_CONFIG with algorithmVersion production-v1 and candidateDimensions=[] for list/detail/archive. T0040 detail validation requires runtime algorithm identity/version/checksum to match the persisted Run. Any real persisted Run created with the approved T0040 config (for example TEST_ALGORITHM_CONFIG/test-v1 during contract setup or future released production config) will fail detail/archive validation through HTTP. Do not use a dummy empty config as the transport contract; derive/use the same approved comparison runtime contract for persisted runs or add a supported production config boundary, with HTTP tests seeding a real Run and proving detail/list/archive work. 2) HTTP tests only cover empty list, not-found detail, and not-found/invalid archive. The brief explicitly requires archive legal request mapping, stale expected conflict 409, cross-workspace 404, and response envelope evidence. Add real HTTP contract tests against an existing persisted Run for list/detail/archive success, archive replay/conflict/stale expected, archived list filtering, and no existence leak. 3) The not_released create test asserts only 424; it does not prove formal create leaves all eight Comparison tables at zero as required. Add a DB count assertion for all comparison_* tables after POST / under production not_released. 4) Test/workspace cleanup is not clean: untracked data/workspaces/ws_test/db.sqlite and data/workspaces/ws_other/db.sqlite remain in the worktree. Brief requires temporary SQLite workspace and no workspace artifacts left in handoff. Clean leaked workspaces and make tests use mkdtemp or guaranteed cleanup, then verify git status for every test workspace path. 5) docs/workpls-absorption-retirement-checklist.md still says W06/T0041 is queued and next step is to implement it; handoff required W06 execution facts. Update it to reflect revision state, but leave completed for controller review only. docs/notes-backend.md also still says W06 is待创建/T0040 approved next, contradicting T0041 handoff. 6) Handoff says canonical backend memory was not found, but the canonical source exists and was updated in the previous review. Worker must read the canonical memory path or accurately report the lookup path attempted. Memory Candidate 'dummy source for read-only endpoints' is rejected because it is not proven safe and is likely masking the runtime config mismatch. Memory Used: External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected rejection of dummy/empty-config read/archive contract; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission affected rejection of claimed archive/not_released/checklist/cleanup coverage. Metadata updated to use_count 47 and 53. W07 remains planned and blocked; do not create it.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 2). Independent validation passed: apps/server typecheck; HTTP contract 32/32; application 113/113; source 70/70; algorithm 15/15; schema 30/30; guard:worktree OK; git diff --check clean; protected paths and leaked ws_test/ws_other/ws_http_test paths clean. Revision 2 closes the behavioral blockers from revision 1: HTTP route now uses a non-empty comparison config; tests seed real persisted runs; list/detail/archive success, archive replay/stale conflict/restore, cross-workspace 404, not_released zero-write, and cleanup are proven. Remaining blocker is scope/contract authorization: T0041 allowed_paths do not include apps/server/src/portrait-comparison/application/ or application/index.ts, but revision 2 adds apps/server/src/portrait-comparison/application/algorithm-config.ts and changes application/index.ts exports to introduce PLS_DEFAULT_ALGORITHM_CONFIG/getProductionQualityPolicy as application-level public surface. The T0041 brief explicitly limited changes to HTTP transport, top-level portrait-comparison index, HTTP test, package script, and docs; it also said application/repository core or shared contract changes must stop and submit CONTRACT_CHANGE_REQUEST. Because this new default algorithm config is a shared runtime contract consumed by HTTP and persisted-run validation, controller must not approve it as an implicit out-of-scope change. Fix within T0041 by either moving the HTTP runtime config into allowed paths without changing application public surface, or submit a clear CONTRACT_CHANGE_REQUEST/brief amendment for the application-level default config/export and then update allowed_paths before handoff. Also update docs to reflect changes_requested after this review. Memory Candidate 'HTTP test seeding pattern' is not promoted until the task is approved. Memory Used: External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected verification of real-run HTTP boundary tests while preserving contract authorization; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission affected acceptance of 32/32 and cleanup evidence. Metadata updated to use_count 48 and 54. W07 remains planned and blocked; do not create it.

Approved revision 3. Scope blocker from revision 2 is closed: algorithm config is route-local within allowed_paths, no application-layer diff remains. Verified actual files and commands: typecheck; HTTP contract 32/32; application 113/113; source 70/70; algorithm 15/15; schema 30/30; guard:worktree; git diff --check; protected paths clean. Production policy remains not_released and formal create zero-writes Comparison tables. Updated batch checklist and backend memory metadata; promoted HTTP real application-seeded fixture lesson.
